items.title: Holocaust survivor says Jews 'have become targets' in Australia

children.content: The Jewish community's experiences of antisemitism have been put on display on the first day of the royal commission triggered by the Bondi Beach terror attack last year.

gallery.altText: Peter Halasz wearing glasses and a dark jacket.

thumbnail.altText: Peter Halasz wearing glasses and a dark jacket.

avatar.alt: Millie Roberts wearing a pink top and earrings and an 'M' necklace

items.title: The treasurer's reality check on Anthony Albanese's bold childcare plan

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has previously committed to delivering universal child care, but Treasurer Jim Chalmers has highlighted concerns about the cost.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese sits at a table talking with children in a child care class

items.title: Iran issues new warning as US launches effort to reopen Strait of Hormuz

children.content: Iran's military has warned US forces not to enter the Strait of Hormuz after US President Donald Trump said the United States would start helping to free ships stranded in the Gulf by the US-Israeli war on Iran.

image.altText: Two cargo ships can be seen in the distance on blue water, against blue skies. Rocky land is jutting out to sea on the left.

items.title: 'Officer safety is non-negotiable': Cops demand answers over trackable tasers

children.content: Police associations and unions demand urgent action over revelations that a security flaw allows officers to be tracked by criminals with a phone or laptop.

articles.title: A hacker built a tool to track police officers. Then he tried to warn them

children.content: A security flaw means the location of devices used by police, potentially including undercover officers and those travelling home, is visible to anyone with a phone or laptop.

image.altText: A man wearing a dark hoodie is silhouetted against the lights of a police station with police cars in front.

gallery.altText: A police officer, wearing a tactical vest with various pieces of equipment including a taser, looks the phone in his hands.

thumbnail.altText: A police officer, wearing a tactical vest with various pieces of equipment including a taser, looks the phone in his hands.

avatar.alt: A headshot of a woman with short blonde hair

items.title: Australia and Japan sign agreements on energy, defence and critical minerals

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and his Japanese counterpart Takaichi Sanae agreed to bolster cooperation on energy, defence and critical minerals, while warning against economic coercion.

image.altText: a female politician wearing a blazer at a table

items.title: Calls for 'compassion' after newborn twin dies at homeless camp

children.content: A women's health advocate says the death of a baby during childbirth at a homeless camp on the Murrumbidgee River is a "terrible day" for the Wagga Wagga community.

image.altText: View of Wagga beach signage from a footbath, looking towards a playground.

items.title: Australian businesses falling victim to new type of fraud

children.content: Higher inflation, a war in the Middle East, and a ban on surcharges are crippling small businesses. Now they are falling victim to a modern crime.

articles.title: Interest rates and recession: What to know before tomorrow's RBA decision

children.content: Business journalist Emily Stewart walks you through the elements that will contribute to the RBA's decision to change the cash rate on Tuesday.

image.altText: Pink and green $100 notes falling

articles.title: What would you like to see in the federal budget?

children.content: We want to know what issues matter to you ahead of the federal budget. Have your say.

image.altText: A graphic showing a range of reader comments about potential issues in Australia's 2025 federal election.

items.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby remembered across NT after 'unthinkable trauma'

children.content: The Northern Territory is continuing to grieve the short life of a five-year-old girl today with crowds in Alice Springs to pay tribute by donning pink, her favourite colour.

image.altText: Two people standing in the water, holding one another with their eyes closed.

items.title: Sydney man accused of murdering parents and brother denied bail

children.content: Jacky Amazing Feng, 32, faced court for the first time on Monday, following the alleged murder of three family members and attempted murder of another.

image.altText: Man with black hair smiling, blurred blue background

items.title: Russian woman's husband beat her but police were no help, so she wrote to Putin

children.content: Domestic violence and abuse have been described as an "epidemic" in multiple countries, but analysts argue there is something that sets Russia apart: the government is making things worse.

image.altText: Close up of a woman who bears the results of being assaulted, with one eye partly closed.

items.title: ABC staff accept enterprise agreement after pay dispute strike

children.content: ABC staff have struck an enterprise agreement with the broadcaster, bringing an end to a pay dispute that culminated in a 24-hour strike.

image.altText: People strike and hold up signs.

items.title: Sports broadcaster Dennis Cometti farewelled at state memorial service

children.content: A host of high-profile Australians gather at Perth Stadium to pay tribute to one of the nation's most well-known sports broadcasters, Dennis Cometti.

gallery.altText: A yellow jacket and a framed picture of Dennis Cometti on a stage in Perth.

thumbnail.altText: A yellow jacket and a framed picture of Dennis Cometti on a stage in Perth.

items.title: AFL umpire Nick Foot's betting agency role over after tribunal case

children.content: Betting agency Sportsbet announces it will no longer feature current sports administrators or officials in their programming "following feedback".

image.altText: AFL umpire, Nick Foot, bends down and tenses before throwing the ball in to restart play during a game.

items.title: It was a record race time. Moments later spectators were suspicious

children.content: Tasmania's top horse race was run 37 metres shorter than advertised. Officials knew the track distance was likely incorrect but proceeded anyway. This is what happened.

image.altText: Elevated view of horse racing event with crowd looking on.

items.title: Rice is cheap, easy to cook and the main ingredient in these four meals

children.content: Rice doesn't need to play second fiddle. The cheap and simple pantry staple can hold up a whole meal.

gallery.altText: Parmesan and balsamic risotto served in a green bowl, garnished with herbs.
Vegetarian fried rice that's baked on a tray with eggs, peas, beans, sugar snap peas, leek and asparagus, an easy family dinner.

thumbnail.altText: Parmesan and balsamic risotto served in a green bowl, garnished with herbs.
Vegetarian fried rice that's baked on a tray with eggs, peas, beans, sugar snap peas, leek and asparagus, an easy family dinner.

items.title: Tim Roth battles Radha Mitchell in gripping Australian action flick

children.content: Shooting in snake-and-spider-infested grass on a Queensland farm, Seven Snipers spooked its stars.

items.title: Busloads of tourists turn quiet street into 'internet sensation'

items.title: Forget the lawn, these mower racers are cutting loose on the race track

children.content: The 2026 All Australian Mower Racing Championships ran for three days over the weekend featuring competitors ready to tear around a track on refurbished ride-ons.

image.altText: A lawn mower race.

items.title: Alleged Anzac Day hecklers face court over 2025 booing

children.content: Witnesses have told a court they were "disgusted" when a group of men interrupted last year's Anzac Day Dawn Service in Melbourne.

image.altText: ANZAC Day dawn service at Shrine of Remembrance

items.title: Three die after suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship

children.content: A suspected outbreak of hantavirus, which is spread by contact with rat and mice faeces, has killed three people on a cruise ship.

image.altText: A cruise ship on the open ocean with a smaller dinghy approaching.

items.title: Ember warning issued for Perth bushfire

children.content: Authorities issue a warning for possible ember attacks as an unseasonal bushfire burns at Herdsman Lake in suburban Perth.

image.altText: A large plume of smoke by a lake in a sunny day.

items.title: Chook left in oven, lost remote among 'absurd' reasons for Triple Zero calls

children.content: Triple Zero call centres are seeing an increase in "absurd" calls, ranging from lost television remote controls to ducks crossing the road.

image.altText: Signage for the Australian emergency call number Triple 000 is seen on the side of a police car

items.title: Daughter hopes $1m reward will solve mystery of mother's disappearance

children.content: Marion Barter has not been seen since 1997. Her daughter is determined to find out what happened to her mother, who she believes may have found herself "in a situation she did not fully control".

image.altText: Woman with dark hair poses with two young children by her side

items.title: China accused of targeting Port Pirie and Hobart metal smelters

children.content: South Australia's treasurer and energy minister says China is undermining Australia's smelting industry and that any shutdown would leave national security "very exposed".

gallery.altText: An industrial site on the edge of a river, as seen from above.

thumbnail.altText: An industrial site on the edge of a river, as seen from above.

items.title: 'Motivation to swim faster': Four people bitten by crocodile before swimming race

children.content: Four people were bitten by a suspected freshwater crocodile just a day before a sold-out swimming competition was run in the same far-north Western Australia lake.

image.altText: A freshwater crocodile resting next to a lake

items.title: A new poll tells us a lot about One Nation's new-found support

children.content: A new poll asks Australians specifically about One Nation and the results show that rather than being a flash in the pan, the minor party's freight train may be a more lasting phenomenon.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson smiling at the camera

items.title: Public servants asked to remove word 'harmful' about SA algal bloom, FOI reveals

children.content: Emails released under FOI laws and obtained by the SA Liberal Party show government workers were removing the word "harmful" from communications about the algal bloom in SA waters, with one team leader labelling it "bizarre".

image.altText: dead, dried up fishes on sand

items.title: Island residents fight land valuations as authorities block building work

children.content: Residents on Phillip Island, where building is limited because of inundation and erosion risk, are battling to have their property valuations reduced.

image.altText: A group of older people, dressed for cold weather, stand in front of a street sign. One lady is holding a little dog.

items.title: Quick hits: Gasly flips out, Verstappen spins and F1 could go back to V8s

items.title: Landlords to lose ability to evict without reason under WA reforms

children.content: Under the proposed laws, landlords will no longer be allowed to terminate a tenant's lease without giving a reason.

image.altText: Tony Buti speaks at a press conference in front of a blue backdrop.

items.title: Saifiti medically retires from league due to shoulder issue

children.content: Daniel Saifiti's stellar career with the Dolphins, Newcastle, NSW, and Fiji comes to a premature end due to injury.

image.altText: A Dolphins ball-carrier in the NRL grimaces as he is tackled by two Gold Coast defenders during a game.

items.title: Socceroos mainstay's injury blow ahead of World Cup, as key defender returns

children.content: US-based Socceroos midfielder Aiden O'Neill suffers an ankle injury less than six weeks out from the start of the FIFA World Cup in North America.

image.altText: New York City footballer Aiden O'Neill is running with the ball during a game

items.title: Broncos release timeline leading to Mariner's 'limb-saving surgery'

children.content: Brisbane Broncos's chief medical officer releases the timeline of Deine Mariner's serious leg injury against the Roosters, leading to "limb-saving surgery" at Sydney's Royal Prince Alfred Hospital.

image.altText: A Brisbane Broncos NRL player runs carrying the ball as a teammate runs inside him.

items.title: Kasatkina wins first tournament as an Australian citizen

children.content: Fighting back from a set down, Daria Kasatkina wins the Catalonia Open final in Spain.

image.altText: Daria Kasatkina plays a double-fisted backhand at the 2026 Madrid Open.
